text:
Once upon a time in a cozy village nestled between lush hills and a sparkling creek, there lived a jolly old owl named Oliver. His feathers were as bright as a starry night sky, and his eyes twinkled with mischief. There wasn’t much wildlife that danced in the moonlight other than raccoons, but even they found occasional amusement at Oliver’s antics.

One lovely spring evening, as stars twinkled above, Oliver decided it was time for a grand party. He rummaged through his pile of acorns until he found what appeared to be the perfect decoration—his splendid silk quilt. Spread out under the glowing asterisks, it seemed incredibly extravagant, and Oliver was sure that birds of every kind would flutter down to admire his patchwork collection.

He fluffed up his feathers elegantly and hooted cheerfully. Guests began to arrive, drawn by the promise of a quiet night filled with music and laughter. At first, it was just a couple of moths playing
